response.justification: Sarah Smith is a capable full-stack and backend software engineer with a strong web and systems programming track record, but her profile is fundamentally misaligned with this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, deep neural network workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design at the silicon level — none of which appear anywhere in her experience. Her closest adjacent work is low-latency software development at Sociomantic and AI-agent tooling at re:cinq, both of which are software-layer concerns, not hardware architecture or performance modeling. There is no evidence of GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD experience, or DNN training/inference optimization.
strengths: Demonstrated performance-aware engineering at Sociomantic, building low-latency systems handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and high-performance databases
Polyglot background including C/C++ and systems-level work (National Instruments LabWindows, embedded platforms), showing some proximity to lower-level programming
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and tooling at re:cinq, indicating awareness of the AI ecosystem even if not at the hardware level
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the foundational technical requirement of this role
No hardware architecture background: no GPU, SIMD/MIMD, deep learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design experience of any kind
Career trajectory is firmly in web and full-stack software engineering; seniority level and depth are not aligned with a Principal IC hardware performance architect role
